video_title: Nvidia CEO Says Tech Stock Selloff Is a Buying Opportunity | The Pulse 6/8/2026
gist: Francine Lacqua's markets show: Nvidia's Jensen Huang calls the AI selloff a buying opportunity as a renewed Israel-Iran exchange and a Houthi Red Sea shipping ban lift oil and complicate ceasefire talks; Goldman's Lindsey Rosner pushes Fed-cut expectations to late-2027 and favors European securitized credit, multiverse's Euan Blair frames an AI 'beneficiaries vs victims' market split and a workforce-reskilling thesis, IATA airline bosses eye consolidation, the UK unveils a £400m chip plan, and Intesa's €31bn Monte dei Paschi bid advances.

themes.theme: AI selloff, bubble debate and the productivity lag

insights.claim: Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang called the AI-related selloff that began last week a buying opportunity, arguing it is a foregone conclusion that AI will become infrastructure for the world.
insights.speaker: Jensen Huang
insights.so_what: A high-profile bullish CEO call into a selloff; note the talk-his-book caveat, but it anchors sentiment for AI-exposed names.
insights.quote: everybody should be very excited. They can now buy stock at a cheaper price

insights.claim: The market is bifurcating into 'AI beneficiaries' versus 'AI victims' (with much of the software world a victim), and positioning around which side a company lands on is the central trade.
insights.speaker: Euan Blair (Multiverse)
insights.so_what: A stock-selection lens for the AI era; software incumbents face disruption risk while enablers/adopters benefit.
insights.quote: the market is splitting into AI victims and AI beneficiaries

insights.claim: AI is currently delivering 'quite limited value to most organizations' despite millions/billions spent on tools and tokens, because the bottleneck is workers rebuilding workflows, not the technology.
insights.speaker: Euan Blair (Multiverse)
insights.so_what: Explains the ROI gap behind bubble fears; adoption/reskilling, not compute spend, is the gating factor for AI productivity gains.
insights.quote: it is basically not really a technology problem

insights.claim: As token costs reprice, the key enterprise question shifts from 'how do I get people using AI?' to 'how do I make AI use purposeful?' — the former can cost money, the latter drives value.
insights.speaker: Euan Blair (Multiverse)
insights.so_what: Signals a move from usage-maximization to ROI-targeted AI deployment; relevant for software/AI-services demand.
insights.quote: how do I make my I use purposeful rather than how do I get people using AI?

insights.claim: Multiverse raised $70m and is expanding in Europe (acquired Germany's largest data company), betting Europe's strong employer-employee social contract forces governments to drive mass reskilling rather than mass layoffs.
insights.speaker: Euan Blair (Multiverse)
insights.so_what: A European structural-tailwind thesis for workforce AI-training businesses tied to labor-protection regimes.

themes.theme: Central banks, rates and credit positioning

insights.claim: Goldman Sachs Asset Management has pushed its Fed rate-cut expectations to mid-to-late 2027 and sees the Fed on hold through year-end, while the ECB, BOE and BOJ are not on hold.
insights.speaker: Lindsey Rosner (Goldman SAM)
insights.so_what: A concrete rate-path call: US hold, no cuts until ~2027; divergence across central banks shapes cross-market rates trades.
insights.quote: We've pushed off our expectations of cuts to mid to next or late next year.

insights.claim: GSAM expects an ECB hike this week but views it as data-dependent and not necessarily followed by another next meeting, with a high likelihood of one more hike by year-end — driven primarily by oil.
insights.speaker: Lindsey Rosner (Goldman SAM)
insights.so_what: The ECB hike is about credibility/inflation control; oil is the swing variable for whether more hikes follow.
insights.quote: really the difference here what's at play is oil

insights.claim: GSAM favors European securitized credit (CLOs), which offer about 20bp more spread than US equivalents, citing positive technicals and Europe's lower software sensitivities.
insights.speaker: Lindsey Rosner (Goldman SAM)
insights.so_what: An actionable credit allocation: European securitized product over US, for incremental spread with favorable technicals.
insights.quote: European loans are in a better place than us

insights.claim: The strong US labor market (3-month payroll average well above 100k) took cuts off the table, but AI euphoria and CapEx-driven wealth effects are now a major driver of US economic strength, with the big productivity gains still to come.
insights.speaker: Lindsey Rosner (Goldman SAM)
insights.so_what: Links AI CapEx to the wealth effect and spending; if AI productivity disappoints, a key support for the US economy weakens.
insights.quote: AI is a huge boon to the US economy

themes.theme: Middle East escalation and energy/shipping risk

insights.claim: Israel struck Iran (Esfahan and a petrochemical plant) hours after Trump told Netanyahu not to retaliate, with Iran's foreign ministry saying the actions complicate peace talks and deepen US-Iran distrust.
insights.speaker: Abeer Abu Omar
insights.so_what: Escalation undermines the MoU that would reopen Hormuz and extend the ceasefire 60 days, keeping a war-risk premium in oil.

insights.claim: The Houthis announced a complete ban on Israeli vessels in the Red Sea, putting a previously immune waterway at risk and threatening to push energy prices further higher.
insights.speaker: Abeer Abu Omar / Anthony DiPaola
insights.so_what: With Hormuz already shut, a Red Sea disruption threatens Saudi Yanbu-coast crude/refined exports — a new upside catalyst for oil.
insights.quote: the Houthis... will issue a ban on all Israeli shipments in the Red sea

themes.theme: Airlines: oil shock, fares and consolidation

insights.claim: IATA's Willie Walsh says it is a 'challenge, not a crisis,' with flying demand robust, but Air France's Ben Smith will not rule out further fare hikes if fuel spikes, which would in turn dampen demand.
insights.speaker: Willie Walsh / Ben Smith (via Guy Johnson)
insights.so_what: Fuel is the dominant variable for airline earnings this summer; fare hikes risk a demand pullback if oil stays elevated.

insights.claim: Strong-balance-sheet groups (IAG, Air France-KLM, Lufthansa) are positioning to consolidate weaker carriers post-crisis, and Gulf carriers (Qatar, Emirates, Etihad) are seeking MORE aircraft delivery slots, expecting to emerge stronger rather than struggling.
insights.speaker: Guy Johnson / Luis Gallego (IAG)
insights.so_what: Counterintuitive: Gulf carriers at the epicenter are grabbing delivery slots ceded by weaker Asian airlines, setting up post-war expansion.
insights.quote: we want more aeroplanes because we see the expansion coming

themes.theme: UK AI/chip policy and digital sovereignty

insights.claim: The UK announced a £400m hardware plan for the state to buy top-tier British-made chips and expand a Scaling Inference Lab, betting on inference (e.g., Fractal) and longer-term photonics (e.g., Onyx) rather than competing in GPU training with Nvidia/AMD.
insights.speaker: Kanishka Narayan (UK AI Minister)
insights.so_what: Signals UK industrial strategy concentrating bets on inference and photonics; relevant to UK chip startups and public-private capital flows.

insights.claim: The UK minister concedes it is unrealistic to win AI infrastructure given industrial energy costs ~3x the US, instead targeting becoming the most AI-adopted G7 nation via a highly educated workforce and a 'third way' on regulation.
insights.speaker: Kanishka Narayan (UK AI Minister)
insights.so_what: Energy costs are the binding constraint on UK data-center/infrastructure ambitions; the bet shifts to adoption, not capacity.
insights.quote: we know energy costs are three times that of the US

themes.theme: Italian banking M&A and IMF on AI inequality

insights.claim: Intesa Sanpaolo's €30.6bn bid for Monte dei Paschi (cash and shares, selling ~half the branches to Unipol) targets control of MPS's Mediobanca stake and Generali stake; Intesa's CEO says it is not a response to Banco BPM and rules out acquiring Generali.
insights.speaker: Tommaso Ebhardt / Carlo Messina (Intesa)
insights.so_what: The strategic prize is the Mediobanca/Generali stakes; an event-driven situation across Italian financials with competing bids.

insights.claim: IMF's Kristalina Georgieva warns the AI revolution risks repeating globalization's inequality and that three regulatory models (European, American, Chinese) are emerging, which the UN should reconcile so they can coexist.
insights.speaker: Kristalina Georgieva (IMF)
insights.so_what: Flags fragmentation in AI governance and distributional risk; a policy backdrop shaping where AI capital and rules concentrate.
insights.quote: three different regulatory models are emerging with regard to artificial intelligence. The European, the American, the Chinese.
